title: mzPeak CV Mapping
description: Describes all associations between a specific schema section and the appropriate CV terms.

model_name.description: Name of the PSI data exchange schema, e.g. mzData, GelML, MIF.

model_uri.description: URI of the data exchange schema.

model_version.description: Version number of the model supported by the CvMapping file.

cv_reference_list.description: Comprehensive list of CVs required by the schema or model.

cv_references.description: One or more CV or ontology resource descriptions.

cv_mapping_rule_list.description: Comprehensive list of associations between the model and CVs.

CvReference.description: Description of a CV or ontology resource.

cv_identifier.description: Short label or namespace abbreviation for the CV; referenced by cv_identifier_ref in CvTerm entries.

cv_name.description: Full descriptive name for the CV.

CvMappingRule.description: Single mapping rule between a specific element of the model and one or more CV terms.

id.description: Unique identifier of the rule within this mapping file.

name.description: Human-readable name for this rule, used for error reporting.

scope_path.description: Element scope in the schema within which the non-repeatable (is_repeatable=false) condition applies.

cv_element_path.description: Path to the element within the scope that holds the CV annotation.

cv_terms_combination_logic.description: Boolean operator describing how multiple cv_terms entries for this rule are combined.

requirement_level.description: Whether the CV association is mandatory (MUST), recommended (SHOULD), or optional (MAY) when the element exists.

cv_terms.description: One or more CV terms allowed to annotate the element addressed by this rule.

CvTerm.description: A single CV term (or subtree) permitted to annotate the element identified by the enclosing CvMappingRule.

cv_identifier_ref.description: Reference to a cv_identifier defined in cv_reference_list, identifying the source vocabulary.

term_accession.description: Accession number of the term in the source CV.

term_name.description: Human-readable name of the term in the source CV.

use_term_name.description: When true, validation checks term_name rather than term_accession.

use_term.description: When true, this term itself may be used to annotate data. When false, only its children (if allow_children is true) are valid.

allow_children.description: When true, child terms of this term are also permitted to annotate data.

is_repeatable.description: When true, this term may appear more than once on the same instance of the associated element.
